seoTitle: Austin ISD Calendar 2026-2027: Holidays, Breaks & PDF
seoDescription: Austin ISD 2026-2027 calendar with Aug 18 first day, May 27 last day, holidays, breaks, staff development days, PDF and ICS.

calendarFaqs.q: When does school start for Austin ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The first day of school for Austin Independent School District in 2026-2027 is Tuesday, August 18, 2026 — one day earlier than the 2025-2026 start of August 19. Austin ISD consistently starts in the third or fourth week of August, later than many North Texas districts. Families arriving from states that begin school in early September should verify the Austin ISD date before booking end-of-summer travel; plan to be back in Austin by August 15 to allow a weekend before the first school day.

calendarFaqs.q: When is the last day of school for Austin ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: The last day of school for Austin Independent School District in 2026-2027 is Thursday, May 27, 2027. The school year covers 180 instructional days between August 18, 2026 and May 27, 2027. Memorial Day 2027 falls May 31 — after the school year has already ended. Summer camps and childcare programs starting after May 27 can align with the Austin ISD calendar without a gap; confirm start dates before registering, as some programs use a June 1 or later start that assumes a longer school year.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Spring Break for Austin ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: Spring Break for Austin Independent School District in 2026-2027 runs Monday, March 15 through Friday, March 19, 2027 — five days. This window almost always overlaps with South by Southwest (SXSW), which typically runs in mid-March. Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (AUS) and downtown hotels price at or near peak demand during the SXSW-Spring Break overlap — families planning out-of-town travel should book by January. Round Rock ISD, Pflugerville ISD, and other neighboring Austin-area districts set their spring break dates independently; verify each district's calendar if coordinating travel across schools.

calendarFaqs.q: Does Austin ISD have school on Good Friday in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: No. Good Friday (March 26, 2027) is a no-school day for Austin ISD students — it falls exactly one week after Spring Break ends on March 19. Easter Sunday is March 28, 2027. The combination of Spring Break (March 15–19) and Good Friday (March 26) creates two separate no-school windows in the same month. Families who did not travel for Spring Break have a secondary long weekend option at the end of March. Neighboring districts may or may not observe Good Friday — verify separately if coordinating with Round Rock or Pflugerville ISD.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Thanksgiving Break for Austin ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: Thanksgiving Break for Austin ISD in 2026-2027 runs Monday, November 23 through Friday, November 27 — the full school week. Students return Monday, November 30, 2026. Additionally, two no-school days fall earlier in November: November 2 (Student/Staff Holiday) and November 3 (Campus Staff Development Day). Families planning fall travel should review the full November calendar, as the combination of the early November no-school days and the full Thanksgiving week creates two distinct no-school windows in the same month.

calendarFaqs.q: When is Winter Break for Austin ISD in 2026-2027?
calendarFaqs.a: Winter Break for Austin ISD in 2026-2027 runs Monday, December 21, 2026 through Friday, January 1, 2027 — 12 days. Students return Tuesday, January 5, 2027; January 4 is an additional no-school day. Austin-Bergstrom International Airport (AUS) sees heavy departure traffic the weekend before Winter Break and the days immediately after New Year's — book holiday flights once the December calendar is confirmed. The final school days before Winter Break (December 18–19) often include end-of-semester activities; confirm with individual campuses before planning early departure.

yearNumbers.label: Starts Earlier
yearNumbers.detail: First day shifts from August 19 (2025-2026) to August 18, 2026 — one day earlier. A single day may seem minor, but it changes the return-from-summer calculation: families need to be back in Austin by Saturday, August 15, rather than Sunday, August 16. Austin-Bergstrom Airport and I-35 are typically congested the weekend before school starts; plan travel return for Thursday or Friday to avoid the back-to-school rush.

yearNumbers.label: Yom Kippur PLED Day
yearNumbers.detail: September 21 is a no-school day (PLED Day observed alongside Yom Kippur) — a shift from 2025-2026, which aligned the Jewish High Holiday closure with Rosh Hashanah on September 23. Austin ISD is one of the few large Texas districts to formally include a Jewish High Holiday on its official no-school calendar. Families who observed the September 23 no-school day in 2025-2026 should note the date moved earlier; the specific alignment varies year to year as the Hebrew calendar shifts relative to the Gregorian calendar.

yearNumbers.label: Eid al-Fitr
yearNumbers.detail: Eid al-Fitr / Parent Conference Day on March 10, 2027 creates a no-school day just five days before Spring Break begins on March 15. For Muslim families, this timing is significant: Eid marks the end of Ramadan and is a major family celebration. The March 10 no-school day combined with Spring Break starting March 15 gives families a natural extended travel window. Austin ISD is one of the few large Texas school districts to formally observe Eid al-Fitr as an official no-school day — check neighboring district calendars separately if coordinating with Round Rock or Pflugerville ISD families.

yearNumbers.label: Good Friday
yearNumbers.detail: Good Friday (March 26, 2027) is a no-school day, falling exactly one week after Spring Break ends on March 19. Families who didn't travel for Spring Break get a long Easter weekend: no school Friday, March 26 through Easter Sunday, March 28. Families planning Easter travel should note that flights departing Thursday evening (March 25) avoid the Friday morning rush at AUS that typically coincides with the spring break week and Good Friday departures. Good Friday is not a guaranteed holiday at neighboring Texas ISDs — Round Rock, Pflugerville, and Leander ISD may or may not observe it; verify each district's calendar separately.

events.name: Indigenous Peoples' Day / Parent Conference Day

calendarNotes:
Austin ISD begins the 2026–2027 school year on Tuesday, August 18, 2026 — one day earlier than the prior year's August 19 start. Texas requires 180 instructional days; Austin ISD's mid-August start is later than most North Texas districts but consistent with the district's historical pattern of beginning in the third week of August. The first weeks of school fall during Austin's hottest weather: August heat regularly exceeds 100°F, and UIL heat protocols restrict outdoor athletics and recess during extreme heat. Families should plan back-to-school shopping early — supply stores near popular campuses fill in the final week before school.

Austin ISD's 2026–2027 calendar includes several no-school days not found on most other Texas district calendars. September 21 is a PLED Day observed alongside Yom Kippur — one of the few major Texas ISDs to align a school closure with a Jewish High Holiday. October 12 is Indigenous Peoples' Day / Parent Conference Day. November brings two consecutive no-school days: November 2 (Student/Staff Holiday) and November 3 (Campus Staff Development Day), shortening that week to just Monday and Tuesday before Thanksgiving. Families tracking fall no-school days should note both the October and early November dates, which fall outside the standard federal holiday calendar.

Spring Break 2026–2027 runs March 15–19, 2027. Eid al-Fitr on March 10 is a no-school day — just five days before Spring Break begins. Good Friday (March 26) is also a no-school day, one week after Spring Break ends March 19. The combination of Eid (March 10), Spring Break (March 15–19), and Good Friday (March 26) makes the second half of March the most concentrated stretch of reduced school days in the second semester. Families planning spring travel should review all three dates. SXSW typically runs in mid-March, overlapping with the Spring Break window: Austin-Bergstrom Airport and downtown hotels reach peak prices during this period. Book Spring Break travel by January to secure reasonable rates.

Thanksgiving Break covers the full school week: November 23–27. Students return November 30. Winter Break runs December 21 through January 1, with students returning January 5 (January 4 is an additional no-school day). Both breaks follow Austin ISD's typical pattern of closing for the full week before and returning after the holiday. AUS departures peak the weekend before Winter Break and the days immediately after New Year's — plan holiday travel once the calendar is confirmed.

The school year closes Thursday, May 27, 2027. Memorial Day 2027 falls May 31 — after school has already ended. High school graduation ceremonies are typically scheduled in late May or early June; individual high school graduation dates are announced by spring, usually by February. Summer programs that start after May 27 can align with the Austin ISD end date; programs starting before May 27 overlap with the final weeks of school.
